fix: comprehensive self-healing for multi-agent groups lost on restart (#412)

## Problem
Multi-agent groups were silently lost across app restarts. The
`organization.json` on disk had:
- All groups with `IsMultiAgent=false`
- All sessions with `Role=Worker` (even orchestrator sessions)
- Multi-agent group IDs from event-diagnostics didn't exist in the file

Affected teams: PR Review Squad, MAUI - PR Reviewer, FixWeirdInfoPList,
PP- Organize, Evaluate Ortinau Skills, Implement & Challenge (6 teams
total, ~25 sessions).

## Root Cause
The self-healing in `LoadOrganization` only checked `Role ==
Orchestrator`, but all sessions defaulted to `Worker` when the org file
was stale. Chicken-and-egg: can't detect orchestrator → can't heal group
→ can't protect sessions → sessions scatter.

## Fix
Three-phase self-healing in `HealMultiAgentGroups()`:

1. **Phase 1**: Detect orchestrator sessions by name pattern
(`*-orchestrator*`) and restore `Role=Orchestrator`
2. **Phase 2**: Restore `IsMultiAgent` on groups that have orchestrator
sessions (with name matching to avoid incorrectly marking repo groups)
3. **Phase 3**: Reconstruct missing multi-agent groups from scattered
sessions by detecting team members via name prefix patterns

Also adds save verification and error logging to
`event-diagnostics.log`.

## Tests
6 new tests covering:
- Orchestrator role detection by name pattern (with suffix variants)
- Group reconstruction from scattered sessions
- Multiple scattered teams handling
- No false positives on regular session names
- No-op when groups are already correct

All 55 organization tests pass.

---------

commit.message:
Fix iOS TestFlight build: remove hardcoded signing identity, clean up plist injection (#411)

## Problem
The iOS build has been failing on TestFlight CI ([run
#23331578098](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/23331578098/job/67864110605))
with:

```
Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8): no identity found
error MSB3073: codesign --force --sign "Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8)" ... exited with code 1.
```

## Root Cause
The csproj had a post-build target (`InjectIOSPrivacyKeys`) with a
**hardcoded codesign exec** using Jakub Florkowski's signing identity.
This was part of a workaround that re-injected NS*UsageDescription
privacy keys into Info.plist after build, then re-signed the app.

## Fix
**Removed the entire PlistBuddy injection mechanism** (both
`InjectMacCatalystPrivacyKeys` and `InjectIOSPrivacyKeys` targets + the
hardcoded codesign).

The injection was a workaround for an old MAUI behavior that stripped
`NS*UsageDescription` keys from the output Info.plist. This is no longer
the case — verified with a fully clean build (`rm -rf bin/ obj/`) on
.NET 10 that the `CompileAppManifest` task in
[`dotnet/macios`](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs)
now preserves all source plist keys.

The privacy keys (`NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`,
`NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription`) are already defined in both
`Platforms/iOS/Info.plist` and `Platforms/MacCatalyst/Info.plist` and
survive the build pipeline untouched.

## Verification
- ✅ Full clean build (bin/ + obj/ nuked) on Mac Catalyst — 0 errors
- ✅ Privacy keys present in output `.app/Contents/Info.plist` without
any injection
- ✅ Confirmed `CompileAppManifest` [source
code](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs#L105)
reads full source plist and doesn't strip NS* keys

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: route ExactDistanceTo* through AABB face prefilter (#144)

* docs: add PR-history lessons to AGENTS.md

Analyzed all 130 pull requests and their reviewer feedback to identify
recurring mistakes made by AI agents. Condense these into concrete rules
across new and expanded sections of AGENTS.md.

Additions to existing sections:
- §1 License: shebang must not precede SPDX header; cmake-format guards
  required for CMake SPDX headers; Jinja2 uses HTML comment style; scripts
  under .github/ covered by SPDX check; Geant4 example files keep their
  original license block with ignore-paths in spdx.yml
- §2 Include Style: add rule against relying on transitive includes
- §4 CMake: PRIVATE for include dirs, PUBLIC for link libs; do not use
  check_cxx_compiler_flag for sanitizer flag probing
- §5 Testing: yaml-cpp required (hand-rolled parsers rejected twice);
  per-fixture CTest split required; disabled tests need programmatic skip
  with explanatory comment and issue link; xfail reclassification must use
  narrow code allowlists; fixture provenance must document all parameters;
  regenerate and commit STEP files after changing generation scripts
- §6 CI: eic_xl:nightly (not eic_ci); cvmfs prerequisite step required;
  no branches: filter on pull_request trigger; git diffs must be generated
  outside the Apptainer container
- §7 Documentation: docstrings must accurately describe implementation;
  docs must be updated in same PR as implementation; code examples must be
  syntactically valid; British spelling required (visualisation not
  visualization)
- §9 Geometry: BRepBndLib::AddOptimal not Add; DistanceToIn/Out returns
  lower bound; GetPointOnSurface must use OCCT tessellation; internal types
  must be private; G4Polyhedra uses circumradius not apothem
- §11 Code Quality: clang-format Standard must be c++20; CSS/JS in separate
  files; codespell notes on Geant4 command names vs prose misspellings

New sections:
- §12 Sanitizers: scope env vars to sanitizer job; narrow suppression scopes;
  no check_cxx_compiler_flag for sanitizer detection
- §13 Report and Script Conventions: scripts in .github/scripts/; shared
  utility module; parsers must be updated with benchmark format changes;
  America/New_York timezone
- §14 Examples: keep original Geant4 license; STEP fixtures centered at
  origin; add Geant4 UI command words to .codespell-ignore instead of
  changing the commands

Also add 'inactivate' to .codespell-ignore since it is a valid Geant4 UI
command (/process/inactivate) quoted in AGENTS.md as an example.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: fix two issues from code review in AGENTS.md

- Correct shebang rule: executable scripts may have a shebang on line 1
  followed immediately by the SPDX header (the required layout for .sh
  files in the repository). The previous wording incorrectly implied
  shebangs must never appear before the SPDX header.

- Clarify xfail error code allowlist rule: point to the canonical source
  (fixture_validation.hh / fixture_validation.cc) for the current list of
  demotable codes, and note that the examples given are illustrative rather
  than exhaustive.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: route ExactDistanceTo* queries through AABB face prefilter

Insights 1 and 5 from callgrind analysis: ExactDistanceToOut and
ExactDistanceToIn both created BRepExtrema_DistShapeShape on the
full shape/compound per query, triggering O(N_faces) calls to
TopExp::MapShapes and BRepBndLib::Add on every DistanceToOut(p)
and DistanceToIn(p) invocation.

Route both through TryFindClosestFace which uses the pre-built
fFaceBoundsCache (AABB per face, constructed once at shape load)
to prune candidates before calling BRepExtrema on individual faces.
For a point near a single face this reduces O(N_faces) BRepExtrema
calls to O(1).

Remove the now-unused DistanceFromPointToShape free function,
fFaceCompound member, and its TopoDS_Compound.hxx include since
ExactDistanceToOut no longer needs the face compound.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add V3, V4, V5 encoding schemes and obstruction strategy pattern (#31)

* feat: add V3/V4 encoding schemes and rename RGB→V1, HSV→V2

- Renamed EncodingScheme.RGB → V1 and EncodingScheme.HSV → V2 throughout
  entire codebase (enums, encoders, builder, director, services, tests)
- Added EncodingScheme.V3: HSV-style channel mapping with no obstruction bar
- Added EncodingScheme.V4: HSV-style channel mapping with obstruction vector
  applied element-wise to the floor-plan bounding box (no bar)
- Introduced Strategy Pattern via ObstructionEncodingStrategy ABC with three
  implementations: ObstructionBarStrategy, NoObstructionStrategy,
  BoundingBoxObstructionStrategy — selected by ObstructionStrategyFactory
- Added HSV_STYLE_SCHEMES frozenset {V2, V3, V4} for HSV pixel override logic
- V3/V4 reuse REGION_CHANNEL_MAPPING_V2; RegionEncoderFactory maps them to V2
- RoomImageDirector now receives encoding_scheme and owns the obstruction strategy
- Added ServiceName.ENCODING_SERVICE_V3/V4 and wired up in Application
- Added comprehensive test suite: tests/components/region_encoders/test_v3_v4_encoding.py
  (20 tests covering factory, V3 bar absence, V4 bbox multiplication, channel mapping)
- All 483 tests pass

* feat: add V5 encoding – single-channel float32 geometric mask

V5 replaces parameter encoding entirely with a fixed-value segmentation mask:
  Background → 0.0, Room polygon → 1.0, Window → 0.6

Key changes:
- EncodingScheme.V5 = "v5" added to enum
- V5_MASK_VALUES constant: {BACKGROUND: 0.0, ROOM: 1.0, WINDOW: 0.6}
- V5ImageDirector (new): builds (H, W, 1) float32 image using existing
  RoomPolygon + WindowRegionEncoder geometry logic; no obstruction bar
- V5EncodingService (new): inherits EncodingService; overrides validation
  to require only room_polygon + window geometry (no reflectances/obstruction);
  returns float32 array without uint8 cast
- ServiceName.ENCODING_SERVICE_V5 added and wired into ServerApplication

* fix: V5 encoding returns correct (128,128,1) float32 with all three values

Two bugs caused V5 to produce wrong output:

1. parse_request injected room-level defaults but not window_frame_ratio,
   which WindowRequest.from_dict() requires. Requests omitting that field
   raised ValueError before V5 encoding could run, causing the server to
   fall back to V2 and return (128,128,4) uint8. Fix: inject
   window_frame_ratio=0.0 default per window in V5.parse_request().

2. _draw_window passed direction_angle and window_geometry (pre-rotation
   objects) to WindowRegionEncoder.get_pixel_bounds(). After facade
   rotation, direction_angle still points in the original world direction,
   so WindowGeometry.window_width_3d projected onto the wrong axis and
   returned ≈0 → zero-height window → 0.6 value never written.
   Fix: strip direction_angle and window_geometry before calling
   get_pixel_bounds so WindowGeometry falls back to max(x-span, y-span),
   which returns the correct 1.2m window width.

Verified: unique output values are now [0. 0.6 1.] and shape is
(128,128,1) float32. All 506 tests pass.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EWmghPbBn3E2xYfawTGpNo

* refactor: decouple V5ImageDirector from WindowRegionEncoder internals

V5 only needs pixel bounds for the window region, which is a purely
geometric operation. Using WindowRegionEncoder (a parameter encoder)
just to call its get_pixel_bounds() was fragile coupling to encoder
internals (_update_parameters, _get_window_bounds).

Replace with a direct call to WindowGeometry.get_pixel_bounds(), which
is the actual source of the computation and already public API:

- Remove WindowRegionEncoder import and class-level _window_encoder attribute
- Remove EncodingScheme import (only used to construct the encoder)
- _draw_window: build a fresh WindowGeometry from the rotated scalar
  coordinates (direction_angle intentionally omitted so window_width_3d
  uses the max(x-span, y-span) fallback) and call get_pixel_bounds()
  directly, then clamp to border

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EWmghPbBn3E2xYfawTGpNo

* refactor: make height/frame-ratio fields Optional in data models

V5EncodingService.parse_request() was injecting fake values for
height_roof_over_floor, floor_height_above_terrain, and window_frame_ratio
to satisfy from_dict() before V5 validation could run. This was a
workaround for a model design issue.

Fix: make those fields truly optional in the shared data models.

RoomEncodingRequest:
- height_roof_over_floor: float → Optional[float] = None
- floor_height_above_terrain: float → Optional[float] = None
- from_dict(): no longer raises ValueError for missing height fields
- validate(): guards height checks with `is not None`
- to_flat_dict(): only includes each field when not None

WindowRequest:
- window_frame_ratio: float → Optional[float] = None
- from_dict(): removed from required-fields list
- validate(): guards frame-ratio range check with `is not None`
- to_dict(): only includes window_frame_ratio when not None

V5EncodingService.parse_request(): reduced to a one-liner `super()` call
— no injection of fake defaults needed.

V1–V4 still receive correct validation: _validate_flat_parameters() in
EncodingService checks for height_roof_over_floor, floor_height_above_terrain,
and window_frame_ratio in the flat params dict, and errors if they are absent.

commit.message:
Replace --ai-command flag with agent-mode subcommand (#300)

`--ai-command` was a top-level flag with no room for additional
configuration. This replaces it with a proper `agent-mode` subcommand
that adds an optional `--system-prompt` parameter.

### Changes

- **`src/lib.rs`**: Removes `ai_command` from `FlylineArgs`; adds
`AgentMode` to `Commands` with `--system-prompt` (optional) and
`--command` (required, multi-value)
- **`src/settings.rs`**: Adds `ai_system_prompt: Option<String>` to
`Settings`
- **`src/app/mod.rs`**: `start_ai_mode()` builds the final subprocess
argument as `"<system-prompt>\n<buffer>"` when a system prompt is
configured, falling back to the raw buffer otherwise

### Usage

```bash
# Equivalent to the old --ai-command
flyline agent-mode --command llm prompt

# With system prompt prepended to the buffer
flyline agent-mode --system-prompt "You are a bash expert. Suggest shell commands." --command llm prompt
```

commit.message:
Add optional path argument to --load-zsh-history (#298)

`--load-zsh-history` always read from `$HOME/.zsh_history` with no way
to specify a custom path.

## Changes

- **`settings.rs`**: Replace `load_zsh_history: bool` with
`zsh_history_path: Option<String>` — `None` = disabled, `Some("")` =
default path, `Some(path)` = custom path
- **`lib.rs`**: Change CLI arg from a boolean flag to `Option<String>`
with `default_missing_value = ""`; update parsing to set
`zsh_history_path`
- **`history.rs`**: `parse_zsh_history()` now takes `Option<&str>`,
falling back to `$HOME/.zsh_history` when `None` or empty

## Usage

```bash
# Existing behavior unchanged
flyline --load-zsh-history

# New: load from a custom path
flyline --load-zsh-history /path/to/.zsh_history
```

Add a validate string parameter (strict/warn/ignore/none) that maps to
the Kubernetes fieldValidation query parameter on Create and Update calls.
Defaults to "strict" to catch schema errors before writing to the cluster.
Works with dryRun=true for a fully non-destructive validation check.

- 5 new e2e subtests in e2e/apply_test.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(drain): add force and timeout parameters to drain_node (#15)

Add two new optional parameters to the drain_node MCP tool:

- force (boolean, default: false): when eviction fails, force-delete the
  pod directly instead of reporting an error. Intended for unmanaged pods
  (not owned by a ReplicaSet, Job, DaemonSet, or StatefulSet) that cannot
  be evicted via the eviction API. WARNING: these pods are permanently lost.

- timeout (float seconds, default: 0 = no timeout): set a deadline for the
  overall drain operation. The deadline is checked at the start of each pod
  iteration; if it has elapsed, the tool returns an error listing the pods
  that were not yet processed. Supports fractional seconds (e.g. 0.5).

Both parameters work alongside the existing ignoreDaemonSets,
deleteEmptyDirData, gracePeriodSeconds, and dryRun parameters. The tool
description is updated to mention the force risk (unmanaged pods lost).

Unit tests added (RED→GREEN):
- force=true deletes unmanaged pods via direct Delete when eviction fails
- force=false reports eviction error for unmanaged pods (existing behaviour)
- timeout fires mid-loop when first eviction blocks past the deadline
- timeout=0 imposes no deadline (no regression)
- force+timeout work together
- force+dryRun does not actually delete pods
- tool description mentions force risk

E2E smoke tests added (dryRun=true, parameter acceptance only):
- force param accepted
- timeout param accepted
- force+timeout together accepted

commit.message:
Store demo GIFs in GitHub release "assets" instead of assets branch (#292)

Demo GIFs were stored in an `assets` branch via an automated PR
workflow. This replaces that approach with a dedicated GitHub release
(`assets` tag) used as a static file host, with each CI run overwriting
the files in-place.

## Changes

- **`.github/workflows/generate-gif.yml`** — Removes the assets-branch
checkout, PR creation steps, and `pull-requests: write` permission.
Replaces them with two commands:
  ```bash
gh release create assets --title "assets" --notes "Demo GIF storage"
2>/dev/null || true
  gh release upload assets *.gif --clobber
  ```
The release is created on first run; `--clobber` handles overwrites on
subsequent runs.

- **`README.md`** — Updates all GIF references from relative
`../assets/*.gif` paths to absolute release download URLs:
  ```
https://github.com/HalFrgrd/flyline/releases/download/assets/<name>.gif
  ```

- **`.gitignore`** — Adds `*.gif` to prevent GIF files from being
committed.

Add --ping-pong flag to create-anim (#290)

Custom prompt animations loop by wrapping back to frame 0 after the last
frame. This adds a `--ping-pong` flag that instead reverses playback
direction at each end, producing a bounce effect.

## Changes

- **`src/settings.rs`** — added `ping_pong: bool` to `PromptAnimation`
- **`src/lib.rs`** — added `--ping-pong` arg to `create-anim`
subcommand; passes value through to `PromptAnimation`
- **`src/prompt_manager.rs`**
- Added `ping_pong: bool` to `ProcessedAnimation`; propagated from
`PromptAnimation`
- `get_frame_spans`: when `ping_pong` is set, uses period `2*(n-1)` so
the frame sequence bounces (e.g. `f0→f1→f2→f1→f0→…`) instead of wrapping
(`f0→f1→f2→f0→…`)
- Added 4 unit tests covering three-frame bounce, two-frame, and
single-frame edge cases

## Usage

```bash
flyline create-anim --name SPIN --fps 10 --ping-pong '|' '/' '-' '\'
# plays: | / - \ - / | / - ...
```

commit.message:
fix: throttle sibling re-resume + faster dead-event-stream detection (#406) (#409)

## Problem

When a session hits a connection error and reconnects, PolyPilot fires
35-47 **concurrent** `ResumeSessionAsync` calls for all sibling
sessions. This floods the server's event delivery mechanism —
`SendAsync` on the primary session succeeds, but the event stream goes
silently dead. The 120s watchdog eventually kills and retries, but the
retry can also be affected.

**Evidence from `~/.polypilot/event-diagnostics.log`** (ci-agentic
session, 2026-03-18):
- 22:56 — watchdog kills after 1173s with no `SessionIdleEvent`
- 22:58 — reconnect fires, retries prompt, logs `"awaiting events"`
- 23:00 — watchdog kills the retry after 120s (`IsProcessing=false —
watchdog timeout after 120s`)

The user had to prod it manually.

## Root Cause

The sibling re-resume `Task.Run` launched all 47 sessions concurrently
with no throttling. The resulting flood of `ResumeSessionAsync` calls
likely exhausted the server's per-client connection limits or I/O
queues, silently breaking the event delivery for the session that just
reconnected.

## Fixes

### 1. Throttle sibling re-resume concurrency (primary fix)
Replace the sequential loop (which was also calling `await` sequentially
— actually serialized, but with no back-pressure) with a proper parallel
dispatch bounded by `SemaphoreSlim(3, 3)`. At most 3 siblings resume at
a time. The primary session's event handler is already registered before
the `Task.Run` starts.

### 2. Faster dead-event-stream detection  
Add `IsReconnectedSend` flag on `SessionState`. On the reconnect path,
set it `true` before `StartProcessingWatchdog`. When the first real SDK
event arrives (`HandleSessionEvent`), clear it. In the watchdog, use a
new `WatchdogReconnectInactivityTimeoutSeconds = 35` (vs 120s) when
`IsReconnectedSend=true` and no tools are involved. This means a dead
event stream after reconnect is detected and retried in ~35s, not 2
minutes.

### 3. Re-snapshot `EventsFileSizeAtSend` on reconnect  
The stale snapshot from the failed primary send is no longer valid after
reconnect. A fresh snapshot enables the watchdog's Case D (dead-send
detection) on the retried send. Also reset
`WatchdogAbortAttempted=false` so Case D can fire on the retry.

## Test Results
- Build: ✅ 0 errors
- Tests: ✅ 2807/2809 passed (2 flaky timing-based tests, confirmed flaky
by running in isolation — both pass)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(run): add ports parameter to run_pod (#18)

Adds a ports parameter to the run_pod tool, allowing users to declare
container ports when creating a debug or one-off pod — enabling
immediate use of port_forward without a separate patch_resource step.

Syntax: comma-separated list with optional protocol suffix:
  "80"              → 80/TCP
  "80,443"          → 80/TCP, 443/TCP
  "8080/TCP,9090/UDP" → explicit protocols

Validation:
- Port number must be 1–65535; returns error otherwise
- Protocol must be TCP, UDP, or SCTP; returns error otherwise
- Empty ports string is accepted (no ports set)

Ports are set on spec.containers[0].ports; no Service is created.

Also adds parsePortsString() helper with full unit test coverage,
and e2e tests verifying containerPort values via kubectl.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(top): add labelSelector parameter to top_nodes (#19)

Adds a labelSelector parameter to the top_nodes tool, enabling users to
filter node metrics by Kubernetes label selector instead of having to
make multiple individual calls for heterogeneous node pools.

Behaviour:
- Lists nodes matching the selector first, then fetches metrics for each
- If no nodes match: returns "no nodes match the given labelSelector" (not an error)
- name and labelSelector are mutually exclusive — returns error if both provided
- Output format unchanged (same per-node CPU/memory summary)

Example:
  { "labelSelector": "node-role.kubernetes.io/worker=" }
  → returns CPU and memory usage for all worker nodes

Also adds testNodeWithLabels() and labelSelectorReactor() test helpers to
support label-aware unit tests with the fake dynamic client (which does not
filter by label selector natively).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Multi head attention (#181)

* Propagate worker subprocess errors through TUI cleanly

Workers now catch exceptions and send formatted tracebacks back
via the metrics pipe instead of printing to stderr (which the Rich
TUI swallows). Main process re-raises as WorkerError, exiting the
Live context so the full traceback prints to the console.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use max instead of mean for combined attention overlay

Shows anything any head cares about, rather than diluting
focused heads with uninterested ones.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Optimize attention: cleaner reshapes, remove redundant CPU transfers

- Reshape Q/K/V directly from conv output to (batch, heads, N, head_dim)
  avoiding non-contiguous intermediates
- Unify train/eval into single code path (dropout only during training)
- Only materialize attention map during eval (skip view reshape in training)
- Remove redundant CPU→CPU transfers in entropy stats computation
- get_attention_entropy() temporarily switches to eval mode for real weights

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* TUI: add SPS, delta column, healthy-range coloring; remove rewards-since-last

- SPS displayed as first metric (standalone row)
- All metrics get a Δ column showing change since last update
- Values outside healthy range (from tensorboard-metrics.md) render in red
- Removed rewards-since-last (superseded by per-metric deltas)
- Per-head attention metrics share parent healthy ranges

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix attention weights: ensure eval mode in debugger

SpatialAttentionPool returns None for attn_map during training mode.
The debugger must switch to eval mode before calling forward_with_attention.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix attention heatmap: normalize per-head views against global range

Individual heads were normalized to their own min/max, making weak
heads appear artificially bright and invisible in the combined view.
Now all views use the global min/max across all heads so brightness
is directly comparable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix worker pool close: catch BrokenBarrierError, not mp.context.TimeoutError

The barrier.wait() raises threading.BrokenBarrierError when a worker
crashes, not multiprocessing TimeoutError. The wrong exception type
caused a secondary AttributeError crash during cleanup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty action mask: honor NES grid_offset tile check bypass

When link_grid_offset != 0, the NES skips Walker_CheckTileCollision
entirely (Z_07.asm:2874). Our tile check was conservative and always
checked, which caused an empty mask crash when Link got pushed into
unwalkable tiles by sword knockback. Now can_link_move returns True
for all directions when grid_offset is non-zero, matching NES behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix device mismatch: move obs/masks to CPU before post-training stats

After training, the network is moved to CPU but mb_obs/mb_masks remain
on CUDA. get_entropy_details and get_attention_entropy then fail when
CoordConv buffers (CPU) are concatenated with the input image (CUDA).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add cross-attention spec: entities query spatial feature map

Design document for entity-spatial cross-attention where entity tokens
from EntityAttentionEncoder query the ImpalaResNet feature map to learn
spatial grounding. Includes visualization design for per-entity heatmaps
in the debugger.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(tools): add create_resource tool (fail if already exists) (#20)

Adds a new `create_resource` MCP tool that uses client.Create() and
returns HTTP 409 Conflict if the resource already exists, in contrast to
`apply_resource` which upserts.

Key behaviour:
- Returns descriptive error on 409: resource "Kind/name" already exists
- Multi-document YAML: creates all docs sequentially, stops on first conflict
- dryRun support (server-side dry run)
- validate parameter (Strict/Warn/Ignore field validation, default Strict)
- Gated behind --allow-write (write rate limiter applied)
- Secret redaction applied to response
- MCP annotations: readOnlyHint=false, destructiveHint=false, idempotentHint=false

Also adds parseManifests() / splitYAMLDocuments() helpers for multi-doc
YAML splitting, shared within the tools package.

commit.message:
Fix infer backward refinement and refresh test goldens (#61)

* Rename array copy to copy_from, fix ident crash on unresolved names, fix prefix FuncName(args) parsing, cown load not readonly

* Dot pass: bind FuncName as zero-arg call when at end of expression (a.method FuncName)

* Deduplicate wrapper primitives using IRSubtype.invariant after alias/shape resolution

* Infer: default-typed literals yield to concrete types at merge points

When merging types from multiple control flow paths (e.g., match join),
a default-typed literal (from an unresolved Const) now yields to any
concrete type from a non-default path. This allows match arms with bare
integer literals to adopt the type from the else arm or downstream
field assignment, enabling literal refinement across match boundaries.

* WIP: DefaultInt/DefaultFloat literal type inference - removes is_default field

Infrastructure complete: DefaultInt/DefaultFloat tokens, is_default() method,
resolve(), merge_type handling, extract_primitive, end-of-pass sweep.
Existing tests pass. Iowise match-arm inference not yet working (WF issue
with TypeName leaking into Const type position during Phase 2).

* WIP: DefaultInt/DefaultFloat - preserve in Const nodes, defer lambda-calling parents for Phase 2

* WIP: DefaultInt - fix sweep for Const/Type contexts, fix extract_primitive, debug reveals merge ordering issue

* WIP: DefaultInt - fix merge_type to only yield to compatible primitives, add New arg default refinement

* WIP: DefaultInt - cascade from New, root cause identified: need cross-function propagation in Phase 2

* WIP: DefaultInt - Phase 2 cross-function propagation attempt (not yet working)

* Lambda grafting: inline lambda inference in parent, cascade refines match arm literals

Lambda apply functions are processed inline by their parent function's
infer convergence loop, sharing the same env. The New arg cascade
refines DefaultInt Consts in grafted lambda bodies to the field type.
Match arm literals like (out) -> 1 assigned to fd: i32 are now
correctly refined to i32.

* Fix: process lambda apply functions both independently and inline

Lambda apply functions need independent processing (for When/cown
return types) AND inline processing (for parent cascade integration).
Removing the Phase 1 skip restores when-lambda behavior while keeping
match-arm cascade refinement working.

* Cleanup: remove dead Phase 2 cross-function code, guard run_cascade

Remove ~200 lines of dead cross-function propagation code from Phase 2
that was superseded by lambda grafting. Add performance guard on
run_cascade to skip when no default Consts exist in env. Restore
DefaultInt reset in Phase 2 for lambda return type re-inference.

* Cache lambda ClassDef lookups in process_function

Build a lambda_cache map at the start of the label loop instead of
traversing the body and scope on every CallDyn. O(1) lookup replaces
O(N) traverse per lambda call per convergence iteration.

* Replace env_fingerprint string construction with direct env comparison

Remove expensive string serialization + sorted map + node cloning from
the Phase B convergence check. Replace with direct env_changed()
comparison that short-circuits on pointer equality. ~20% improvement
on the match_value_infer test (23s → 19s).

* Optimize run_cascade: index-based queue propagation

Replace O(iterations * labels * statements) full-rescan cascade with
O(changed_locations * fanout) index-based queue propagation. Build
reverse index (source location → statements) once per process_function,
reuse across all cascade calls.

* Use Subtype for type comparison in infer pass

- Move DefaultInt/DefaultFloat token definitions to lang.h
- Add DefaultInt/DefaultFloat axioms (AxiomEq + no-contradiction) to subtype.h
- Replace ad-hoc types_equal() with Subtype.invariant() throughout infer.cc
- Remove fast_merge_type(); Phase B uses merge_type() with init_env skip optimization
- Remove debug timing instrumentation
- 10x speedup on match_value_infer (15s -> 1.5s)

* Simplify infer pass: table-driven handlers, consolidated post-convergence, delta envs

- Table-driven dispatch for 35 trivial statement handlers (4 tables replace ~120 lines of copy-paste else-if branches)
- Delete unused merge_env function
- Consolidate 3 post-convergence label iterations (Const finalization, NewArrayConst finalization, TypeAssertion cleanup) into one pass
- Extract finalize_const lambda to deduplicate Const finalization between main and lambda paths
- Delta branch_exits: store only narrowed entries instead of full env copies
- Delta exit_envs: store only entries that differ from init_env baseline
- Net reduction: 4425 -> 4293 lines (-132)

* Fix infer_backward regression, rename match_type_error to match_value_match, regenerate golden files

- Fix Phase 4 backward refinement: check call_node instead of is_default()
  for triggering backward refinement of prior calls. The call result type
  may be a concrete wrapper (e.g., wrapper[u64]) even when the inference
  was from default-typed literals.
- Rename match_type_error to match_value_match: with DefaultInt inference,
  the match arm literal is correctly inferred as i32, so the test now
  succeeds instead of producing a type error.
- Regenerate all golden files for DefaultInt/DefaultFloat changes.
- 210/210 compile+run tests pass.

* Fix generic_when: only graft lambda bodies that have default-typed literals

Lambda grafting processes lambda bodies inline during the parent function's
process_label_body. This enables bidirectional type refinement for DefaultInt
literals. However, grafting can pollute the lambda's AST with stale
intermediate types from the parent's env, causing incorrect return type
inference when the lambda is later processed independently.

Fix: only graft when the lambda body actually contains DefaultInt/DefaultFloat
Const nodes that need refinement from the parent context. Lambdas without
default literals (like match-arm lambdas in generic when blocks) are left
to their own process_function for correct independent type inference.

212/212 compile+run tests pass.

* Remove lambda grafting, improve When param propagation and TypeArgs re-inference

- Remove CallDyn lambda grafting (inline lambda body processing). It
  modified the original lambda's AST, causing incorrect return types
  for lambdas in generic contexts. The deferred loop handles
  cross-function type propagation instead.
- Remove lambda_cache infrastructure (no longer needed).
- When handler: unconditionally set lambda params from cown types
  (not just when TypeVar). This ensures params have concrete types
  when the deferred loop re-processes the lambda.
- TypeArgs re-inference: when existing TypeArgs contain TypeParam
  references (from a prior pass with generic types), re-infer them
  from actual arg types. This enables lambda classes created with
  generic args to get concrete TypeArgs after cown type propagation.
- has_typevar: check for DefaultInt/DefaultFloat Const nodes in
  function body, not just return type. Ensures when-lambda bodies
  with unresolved literals are deferred for re-processing.

212/212 compile+run tests pass. generic_when still fails (pre-existing
issue requiring generic lambda instantiation in the deferred loop).

* Remove incorrect When return type propagation from enclosing function

The When handler was propagating the enclosing function's return type
to the when-lambda's apply. This is wrong: the when-lambda's return
type T determines the When result as cown[T], and T must be computed
from the lambda's body, not from the enclosing function.

In generic contexts, the lambda body may contain unresolved method
calls on TypeParams. The infer pass correctly leaves the return as
TypeVar for the reify pass to resolve after monomorphization.

generic_when still fails due to a reify pass function processing order
issue: lambda::apply is processed before lambda::apply's return
type is resolved.

* Add infer pass design document

Bidirectional dataflow analysis with per-label environments and merge-based
type lattice. Replaces Phase A/A.5/B, lambda grafting, and cascade with a
single fixpoint loop. Each statement has a transfer function with forward
and backward components, all using merge. ~1080 lines estimated vs 4280
current.

* Final infer pass design doc with implementation prompt

Complete rewrite guide for the next session. Includes:
- Implementation prompt with step-by-step instructions
- Which helpers to keep vs rewrite
- Critical details for call_node and Copy/Move backward merge
- All transfer functions spelled out
- Case verification table
- Size estimate (~1640 lines with kept helpers)

* Complete infer pass design doc: add missing transfer functions

Add ConstStr, Convert, TypeAssertion, FFI, NewArray/NewArrayConst,
ArrayRef variants, RegisterRef, Typetest, and unary ops. Clarify When
handler (navigate ClassDef, set params unconditionally with merge).
Note tuple tracking as kept helper for heterogeneous tuple semantics.

* WIP: Rewrite infer pass with worklist-driven bidirectional dataflow

- Replace 4279-line phased implementation with 2466-line worklist algorithm
- Separate forward envs (predecessors→successors) and backward envs
  (successors→predecessors) eliminate oscillation
- TypeVar added to Subtype axioms (AxiomEq + AxiomFalse)
- DefaultInt yielding checked before Subtype.invariant
- merge_type returns {} for no-change (pointer-based convergence)
- Union default replacement in merge_type
- 50/72 exit codes correct (22 remaining), 0.5s per test
- No crashes (was 26), all failures are typecheck errors

* Improve infer propagation and CallDyn resolution

commit.message:
perf: cache per-face AABBs to prefilter TryFindClosestFace (#125)

* perf: cache per-face AABBs to prefilter TryFindClosestFace

Callgrind profiling on g4box-box-20x30x40-v1 (6 faces, 2048 rays)
showed TryFindClosestFace (called from SurfaceNormal) accounted for
~45% of total instruction count in the baseline (8.4B Ir). It called
BRepExtrema_DistShapeShape(vertex, face) once per face unconditionally,
giving O(N_faces) expensive evaluations per query.

The face-by-face loop cannot be replaced with a solid-wide query: on
periodic surfaces (torus seam) the solid-wide BRepExtrema query may
return an edge-supported solution with ambiguous UV parameters, leading
to incorrect normals. The face-by-face approach is retained for
correctness, but augmented with an O(1) AABB lower-bound prefilter.

Cache a per-face Bnd_Box in fFaceBoundsCache (populated once in
ComputeBounds()). Before each BRepExtrema_DistShapeShape call, compute
the distance from the query point to that face's bounding box: if this
lower bound is >= the current best distance, the face cannot be closer
and is skipped. For convex shapes this prunes all but the nearest face
once the first match is established, reducing BRepExtrema calls from
N_faces to ~1-2 per query.

Callgrind comparison on g4box-box-20x30x40-v1:
  Baseline:  8,443,916,460 Ir (100%)
  Change B:  5,908,640,215 Ir  (-30%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: replace DistanceToOut(p) face loop with solid-wide BRepExtrema query (#124)

The scalar DistanceToOut(p) previously iterated over all N faces,
calling BRepExtrema_DistShapeShape(vertex, face) once per face.
Callgrind profiling on g4box-box-20x30x40-v1 (6 faces, 2048 rays)
showed this accounted for ~49% of total instruction count.

Replace with a single BRepExtrema_DistShapeShape(vertex, fFaceCompound)
call, where fFaceCompound is a TopoDS_Compound of all faces cached at
construction time in ComputeBounds().  OCCT builds an internal
bounding-box acceleration tree over the compound and evaluates only
necessary candidates, reducing per-query work from O(N_faces) full
evaluations to O(log N_faces) + evaluation of close candidates.

Note: the query must target the face compound, not the solid itself.
BRepExtrema_DistShapeShape(vertex, solid) returns distance zero for
interior points because the solid contains the vertex; the face
compound has no interior volume and yields the correct surface distance.

Callgrind comparison on g4box-box-20x30x40-v1:
  Baseline:  8,443,916,460 Ir (100%)
  Change A:  5,918,336,129 Ir  (-30%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: --dump-logs accepts optional path; --stream-logs mentions stderr (#285)

`--dump-logs` was a boolean flag with no way to specify the output file.
`--stream-logs` didn't surface that `stderr` is a valid special-case
value.

## Changes

- **`--dump-logs[=<PATH>]`**: Changed from a boolean flag to an
optional-value argument. Without a path, retains existing behavior
(auto-generates `flyline_logs_<pid>.txt` in CWD). With a path, writes to
the specified file.
  ```bash
flyline --dump-logs # → flyline_logs_12345.txt (as before)
  flyline --dump-logs=/tmp/my_logs.txt   # → /tmp/my_logs.txt
  ```

- **`--stream-logs <PATH>`**: Updated doc comment to explicitly mention
`stderr` as a valid value for streaming logs to standard error.

- **`logging::dump_logs()`**: Updated signature to `dump_logs(path:
Option<PathBuf>)`, using the auto-generated path when `None`.

- **`flyline_help.txt` / `README.md`**: Updated generated help text to
reflect both changes.

commit.message:
Fix empty benchmark report after Google Benchmark migration (#122)

* Initial plan

* Fix empty benchmark report by piping through scripts/bench_report.py

After migrating bench_navigator to Google Benchmark (#117), the CI was
not updated to use the new JSON-based pipeline. The fix adds
--benchmark_format=json so that:
- Google Benchmark JSON goes to stdout, piped through scripts/bench_report.py
- The custom navigation summary goes to stderr (visible in CI logs)
- scripts/bench_report.py converts JSON to the text format expected by
  generate_bench_report.py

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix JSON corruption by writing benchmark output to file instead of piping stdout

Using --benchmark_format=json caused stdout to be contaminated by
Geant4/OCCT messages written during benchmark execution, resulting in
a JSONDecodeError in scripts/bench_report.py.

Use --benchmark_out=bench_raw.json --benchmark_out_format=json to write
the JSON to a file, then pass the filename to scripts/bench_report.py
instead of piping from stdin.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>
